inventory_reconciliation.notes: P2 and P4 are currently devoid of their registered specimens. The soil in the lower black pot (P3/P4 zone) shows only a single, isolated leaf structure, suggesting a collapse of the original multi-plant configuration.

plant_audit.p2_mexican_mint: Systemic Loss: No viable plant matter detected.
plant_audit.p3_pothos: Stressed: One large necrotic leaf (yellowing/browning), one healthy leaf remaining. Rabbit anchor remains stable.
plant_audit.p4_silver_guest: Systemic Loss: No viable plant matter detected.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently damp, dark, and granular. No significant fungal blooms observed.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Presence of white perlite/debris on the soil surface of P3. The lower pot contains a single, isolated green leaf that appears to be a remnant of P3 or a failed propagation attempt.

temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-3: Minor leaf tip browning on P3.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_t-1: P3 leaf drooping; P2/P4 soil remains barren.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Stasis in decay; no new growth or recovery.

anomalies: Total loss of P2 and P4 specimens
Unidentified isolated leaf in the lower black pot
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is in a state of significant decline. The Pothos (P3) is exhibiting advanced signs of physiological stress, characterized by severe leaf yellowing and necrosis. The Mexican Mint (P2) and Silver Guest (P4) have suffered systemic loss, leaving their respective pots barren. The environment appears to be suffering from either over-saturation or a failure to thrive in the current indoor lighting conditions. The white rabbit anchor remains the only constant in an otherwise deteriorating botanical environment.
